name: Oglufanide
description: Oglufanide is a synthetic dipeptide immunomodulator in development for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C viral infection. Oglufanide was originally developed to treat severe infectious disease in Russia (where it is a registered pharmaceutical), and was extensively studied in cancer clinical trials in the United States before being acquired by Implicit Bioscience in 2005. Oglufanide works as a regulator of the body's immune response, is being given by intranasal administration to patients with chronic hepatitis C viral infection.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in hepatitis (viral, C).
pharmacodynamics: Oglufanide regulates the body's innate immune response to defeat invading germs and cancer cells. It may control or reverse the suppression of the immune system which the hepatitis virus uses to defeat our normally healthy defenses.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Oglufanide.

interactions.name: Paclitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Oglufanide.

interactions.name: Docetaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Docetaxel is combined with Oglufanide.

interactions.name: Cabazitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Cabazitaxel is combined with Oglufanide.
